title: The Inheritance
subtitle: a spectacular gift and a frightening implication
bullets: Isaac Newton's Principia of 1687 showed one simple law governs both the fall of an apple and the orbit of the Moon, the gift the whole movement inherited.
The standard image of the age was the clock: a lawful mechanism with no purposes, understood by taking it apart, not by asking what it wants.
The frightening implication: if reason can decode the physical universe, why not government, rights, wealth, law, and God too.
The philosophes were writers and public intellectuals, not academic philosophers; their enemy word was superstition, their friendly word tolerance.
Kant's later motto sapere aude, dare to know, locates the Enlightenment not in a conclusion but in the nerve to use your own head.
The Atlantic slave trade reached its highest volume during the very decades of the declarations of rights, financed in the cities where the salons met.
The same universalism that produced human rights also produced the first attempts to sort humanity into a hierarchy of races by measurement.
None of it happened in a calm world: this is also the story of a book-smuggling operation that worked against censors, kings, and churches.

title: The English Laboratory
subtitle: a king on a leash and a theory of the blank mind
bullets: Louis XIV's Versailles, lettres de cachet, and 1685 revocation of the Edict of Nantes made absolute monarchy the model the Enlightenment defined itself against.
The Glorious Revolution of 1688 deposed James II and the English Bill of Rights hired William and Mary on written, enforceable terms.
John Locke's Two Treatises of Government argued government is a revocable contract for protecting natural rights, and revolution is legitimate when it breaks.
Locke's A Letter Concerning Toleration argued belief cannot be coerced, so the state should protect life and property, not save souls.
Locke's Essay Concerning Human Understanding said the mind begins as blank paper and all knowledge comes from experience, the doctrine of empiricism.
No innate ideas meant nothing in a person is fixed by birth: change the environment and you change the person, the seed of progress.
By 1700 England was a proof of concept with arguments powerful enough to be turned against the people who first made them.

title: The Republic of Letters
subtitle: the network that could not be decapitated
bullets: The Republic of Letters was an uncentralized international web of writers and readers; a working philosophe might send and receive thousands of letters.
The French salon, run by women like Marie-Thérèse Geoffrin and Julie de Lespinasse, was an unlicensed university where a commoner and a duke talked as equals.
The London coffeehouse, the penny university, let any literate man argue across rank over caffeine rather than beer.
Every continental state required a censor; dangerous books were printed in the Dutch Republic and smuggled back in false-bottomed crates.
Robert Darnton's reconstruction of a Swiss publisher's ledgers showed the clandestine list mixed philosophy with pornography and libel.
Pierre Bayle's Historical and Critical Dictionary hid heresy in vast footnotes and argued a society of atheists could be perfectly moral.
There was no head to cut off: you could jail Voltaire or burn a book, and the system simply routed around it.

title: The Newtonian Gospel
subtitle: turning a proof into a faith
bullets: Newton's Principia was almost unreadable; the people who turned it into a popular gospel were mostly not scientists.
Newton declined to say what gravity is, and to followers of René Descartes action at a distance sounded like a relapse into magic.
For forty years on the Continent Descartes's vortices beat Newtonian gravitation on grounds of common sense.
Dutch demonstrators like Willem 's Gravesande turned Newtonian law into paying public theater with orreries and electrostatic machines.
Voltaire's Elements of the Philosophy of Newton popularized the system in France, with the mathematics supplied by Émilie du Châtelet.
Du Châtelet produced the standard French translation and commentary of the Principia while barred by sex from every academy.
Alexander Pope's couplet, Let Newton be! and all was light, is the literal source of the light in Enlightenment.
The century tried to clone Newton into every domain: the conviction that society has a hidden lawful order is its central, contestable bet.

title: Voltaire
subtitle: the writer as a one-man check on church and crown
bullets: François-Marie Arouet reinvented himself as Voltaire and smuggled subversion inside delight, influential because he was an entertainer, not despite it.
Beaten by a nobleman's servants and then thrown into the Bastille for seeking redress, he learned privilege outranked justice.
The Lettres philosophiques (1734) praised English liberty so brightly that every sentence was, by silent arithmetic, an indictment of France.
He worked fifteen years at Cirey with Émilie du Châtelet and feuded with Frederick the Great before settling at Ferney by the Swiss border.
Candide (1759) marched its hero through war, slavery, and the Lisbon earthquake to demolish philosophical optimism by accumulation.
The campaign to exonerate the executed Jean Calas invented the modern public intellectual using publicity to overturn a judicial atrocity.
His war-cry, crush the infamous thing, aimed not at religion as such but at organized fanaticism allied to state power.

title: The Science of Society
subtitle: Montesquieu builds a machine that stays free
bullets: Montesquieu's Persian Letters made France look like an absurd foreign custom, the relativizing move at the root of social science.
The Spirit of the Laws sorted governments by their driving principle and argued laws must fit a society's size, economy, and conditions.
Its climate argument was empirically wrong and the respectable ancestor of later climate-and-race determinism, the same impulse that founded social science.
The separation of powers is an engineering idea: split legislative, executive, and judicial power so the system stays free even when staffed by the corrupt.
Forty years later a group of men in Philadelphia built a government from this blueprint and cited Montesquieu by name.
Montesquieu was no democrat; he wanted liberty under law and the parlements strengthened as brakes on the crown.
His relativism cuts against the universalism of the later declarations, a tension the Enlightenment never resolved.

title: The Great Machine
subtitle: a reference book engineered to rewire authority
bullets: Denis Diderot and Jean le Rond d'Alembert turned a translation job into the Encyclopédie, a twenty-eight-volume map of everything known and done.
D'Alembert's Preliminary Discourse placed theology inside one human, evidence-based tree of knowledge: the radicalism is in the table of contents.
The cross-references were the subversion: a bland pious entry pointed to superstition or prejudice, and the censor could not censor a connection.
Hundreds of engraved plates documenting crafts made a political statement disguised as a diagram: useful knowledge is real knowledge.
Suppressed by the royal council and Indexed by the Church, the work nearly broke when d'Alembert quit and Diderot carried on alone.
The censor Malesherbes tipped Diderot off to a raid and sheltered the manuscripts, proof the Enlightenment had friends inside the machine.
By 1772 it modeled, at industrial scale, a world where nothing is off-limits to questions, owned by no priest and no king.

title: The Enemy Within
subtitle: Rousseau turns the method against the movement
bullets: Jean-Jacques Rousseau had the keys to the salon and hated the room: the Enlightenment's great insider-outsider.
His Discourse on the Sciences and Arts argued the progress of civilization had corrupted morals, not improved them.
The Discourse on the Origin of Inequality located the fall in the first man who enclosed ground and said this is mine.
The Social Contract opens man is born free, and everywhere he is in chains, and makes sovereignty belong inalienably to the people.
Its solution, the general will, is the deepest argument for popular self-rule and the source of the chilling phrase forced to be free.
Émile argued the child is naturally good and deformed by society; the Confessions made the authentic inner self the seat of truth.
That answer, feeling and nature against a deforming civilization, is the seed of Romanticism, the reaction that defined itself against the Age of Reason.

title: The Hotbed of Genius
subtitle: a poor country's cheap universities
bullets: A visitor on Edinburgh's High Street could reportedly take fifty men of genius by the hand: the Scottish Enlightenment.
Its causes were unglamorous: cheap broad universities, a small networked elite, a Moderate church, and the burning question of how a poor society gets rich.
David Hume turned skeptical empiricism on reason itself, showing we never observe the causal link and live by custom and habit, not proof.
Conjectural or stadial history argued a society's whole structure follows from its mode of subsistence, the ancestor of sociology.
Ibn Khaldun had built a structural science of society four centuries earlier, a precedent the Scots did not know they had.
The same single ladder from savage to commercial that founded social science also furnished a respectable theoretical frame for empire.
James Hutton's geology found no vestige of a beginning, no prospect of an end, opening deep time under the century's feet.

title: The Wealth of Nations
subtitle: the Newton of wealth, and what he actually said
bullets: Mercantilism held that wealth is a hoard of gold and trade a zero-sum war, justifying monopolies, tariffs, and captive colonies.
The Physiocrats led by François Quesnay treated the economy as a natural system and coined laissez-faire, handing both ideas to Smith.
Smith's Theory of Moral Sentiments grounded morality in sympathy, the half you need before the market book makes sense.
The Wealth of Nations (1776) redefined wealth as production, making the division of labor the engine, illustrated by a pin factory.
Pursuing private gain, people are led by an invisible hand to an end no one intended: spontaneous order.
The caricature of greed-is-good would have appalled Smith, who attacked businessmen and the great chartered monopolies.
He was scathing about the East India Company, which after Plassey ran Bengal as a private extraction operation that helped cause a famine.
Smith founded the science later used to argue against everything he cared about, an Enlightenment pattern by now.

title: God on Trial
subtitle: a prosecution divided against itself
bullets: Most philosophes were not atheists but deists, keeping a rational creator who set the world-machine running and then stepped back.
The deists' fortress was the argument from design: a watch implies a watchmaker, so a cosmic mechanism implies a cosmic designer.
Spinoza had already argued the Hebrew Bible was a composite human document, the underground root of the period's biblical criticism.
Hume's Of Miracles showed no testimony can ever outweigh the uniform experience that is a law of nature.
Hume's Dialogues Concerning Natural Religion, published after his death, dismantled the design argument from inside.
From Baron d'Holbach's salon came the boldest books: La Mettrie's Man a Machine and d'Holbach's atheist System of Nature.
Even Voltaire recoiled from the materialists: the fiercest argument about God was inside the Enlightenment, not against the Church.
Toleration and impersonal-cosmos arguments were old in Mughal, Islamic, Indian, and Chinese thought; what was new was their public printing.

title: The Philosopher Kings
subtitle: reform from above, and why it failed
bullets: Enlightened absolutism tested a tempting hypothesis: capture the despot instead of overthrowing him, and let reform descend from the throne.
Frederick II of Prussia abolished torture and hosted Voltaire at Sanssouci but kept serfdom and seized Silesia: reason served Prussian power.
Catherine II of Russia bought the philosophes' loyalty, even funding Diderot, while serfdom was extended and intensified under her.
Catherine's Instruction lifted Montesquieu and Cesare Beccaria, whose On Crimes and Punishments founded modern criminal-law reform.
After the Pugachev rebellion she turned repressive and exiled Radishchev for a travelogue exposing serfdom.
Joseph II of Austria genuinely tried, abolishing serfdom by decree across the Habsburg lands, and it broke in his hands.
The experiment reported back: unaccountable power reforms only to the edge of its own power and then stops, dies, or turns.

title: The Aufklärung
subtitle: Kant defines the movement and draws its edge
bullets: The German Aufklärung ran in a professorial key, reconciling reason, faith, and the inner moral life rather than crushing the Church.
Gotthold Ephraim Lessing and the Jewish philosopher Moses Mendelssohn were close friends across the deepest religious divide.
Lessing's Nathan the Wise and its ring parable argued true religion is shown in loving conduct, not doctrine.
Mendelssohn's Jerusalem argued for separating religion from state coercion, founding Jewish modernity against the disabilities he lived under.
Answering a Berlin magazine contest, Kant defined enlightenment as emergence from self-incurred immaturity, with the motto sapere aude.
Kant's Critique of Pure Reason argued the mind structures experience, so we know the world only as it appears, never in itself.
The Age of Reason's greatest philosopher used reason to fix exactly how far reason can go, the movement's most self-aware act.

title: The Half-Light
subtitle: the universalism tested against its own conduct
bullets: The eighteenth century's great declarations of liberty coincided with roughly six million people shipped in the Atlantic slave trade.
Some philosophes condemned slavery and moved on; Locke helped draft slave-colony Carolina and held shares in the Royal African Company.
The same classifying impulse that produced Linnaeus's taxonomy produced the first systematic scientific racism, ranking humans with Europeans on top.
Women built and ran the salon, the movement's central institution, and Émilie du Châtelet produced the standard French Principia, while barred from every academy.
Rousseau's Émile prescribed training girls out of the autonomy men were urged to dare.
Mary Wollstonecraft's A Vindication of the Rights of Woman refuted the philosophes with their own logic, and the weapons worked.
The Enlightenment was not the dawn with a few shadows but a half-light, brightness and darkness from the same source.

title: Reason in the New World
subtitle: the library goes outside
bullets: American colonists treated Locke and Montesquieu as an engineering manual and built a working government from the blueprint.
No taxation without representation was the Two Treatises applied to a customs dispute.
Thomas Paine's Common Sense argued against monarchy itself in plain prose and sold in numbers no American book has matched since.
Thomas Jefferson's Declaration of Independence is Locke versified, drafted by hands that also wrote slavery into the new republic.
The 1787 Constitution is Montesquieu built: three powers set against each other so the structure stays free even when staffed by the corrupt.
James Madison's Federalist line, if men were angels no government would be necessary, is the Enlightenment view of human nature applied to plumbing.
Europe watched: a real people had declared rights, fought, won, and built a rational republic, collapsing critique into program.
The Marquis de Lafayette fought under Washington, returned a convert, and would help draft the French Declaration five years later.

subtitle: the ideas meet a bankrupt monarchy
bullets: It did not start as a revolution; it started as a bankruptcy, a state that could not tax the privileged orders.
Cornered, Louis XVI summoned the Estates-General for the first time since 1614, convening the body that would dissolve his sovereignty.
The cahiers de doléances showed a society that had absorbed a working vocabulary of rights, equality, and consent.
Sieyès's What Is the Third Estate? answered the Revolution in three beats: everything, nothing, to become something.
The Third Estate declared itself the National Assembly and swore on a royal tennis court not to disband until France had a constitution.
On 14 July 1789 a crowd stormed the Bastille; the Great Fear followed and the night of 4 August abolished feudalism and privilege.
The Declaration of the Rights of Man distilled the century to one page, addressed to all humanity with no border.
That universal all was voted by a nation whose colonies held half a million enslaved people: triumph and contradiction in the same text.

title: The Republic of Virtue
subtitle: the project that killed its idealists
bullets: War with the monarchies of Europe transformed everything: a besieged camp does not do due process.
The monarchy fell, a Republic was proclaimed, and the king was guillotined in January 1793.
The Marquis de Condorcet wrote the most optimistic Enlightenment book, the Sketch of the Progress of the Human Mind, hiding from the Revolution that killed him.
Robespierre was not a cynic but a sincere disciple of Rousseau building a Republic of Virtue on the general will.
Rousseau's forced to be free stopped being philosophy and became a judicial procedure: perhaps seventeen thousand formally executed.
The machine consumed its own operators until it reached Robespierre and guillotined him too in July 1794.
The Republic guillotined the chemist Antoine Lavoisier and staged the deist Festival of the Supreme Being in the same months.
Whether that was the Enlightenment betrayed or unmasked is the question the book refuses to answer cheaply.

title: The Rights of Man at the Limit
subtitle: the sentence tested without an exemption clause
bullets: Saint-Domingue was the most profitable colony on Earth, running on the lethal exploitation of half a million enslaved Africans.
Asked whether all men included the half-million, Paris made its answer clear enough, so the enslaved answered for themselves.
The Haitian Revolution (1791–1804) was a thirteen-year war against the planters, Spain, Britain, and Napoleon's largest expedition.
Toussaint Louverture, born enslaved, became one of the age's foremost generals and died in a freezing French prison cell.
In 1804 Haiti became the first nation created by a successful revolt of the enslaved, the sentence tested and found to have no exemption clause.
France abolished slavery in 1794 under the pressure of revolt, and Napoleon reimposed it in 1802.
France later extorted from Haiti a crushing indemnity, payment from the formerly enslaved to compensate their former enslavers.
Britain's abolitionists, including Olaudah Equiano and William Wilberforce, won abolition of the slave trade in 1807.

title: Reason in Uniform
subtitle: the Enlightenment's executor and undertaker
bullets: Napoleon Bonaparte rose only in a France that had abolished birth as the qualification for office, then ended the Republic in 1799.
Crowned Emperor in 1804, he took the crown from the Pope's hands and set it on his own head.
The Napoleonic Code gave a single rational civil law, the direct ancestor of the law of dozens of countries.
He entrenched the metric system and where his armies marched they swept away serfdom, feudal courts, and ghetto walls.
He reimposed slavery in 1802 and sent the army that died trying to re-enslave Haiti: equality stopped at the color line.
His universalism by occupation called up its opposite: Herder, Goethe, Romanticism, and modern nationalism.
The retreat from Moscow in 1812 destroyed the Grande Armée; Waterloo in 1815 finished him.
The Congress of Vienna restored kings to thrones but could not put the ideas back in the books.

title: The Argument
subtitle: a verdict still genuinely open
bullets: The Enlightenment is the rare subject whose verdict is still open, argued by serious people who are not cranks.
The threshold question is whether there was even one Enlightenment: one identifiable nerve, sapere aude, but many bodies.
For the defense: consent of the governed, rights against the state, separation of powers, the secular state, and the abolition of torture.
Even its catastrophes were indicted in its own language: abolition used universal rights, Wollstonecraft universal reason, Haiti the Declaration verbatim.
The conservative count, Edmund Burke, says rational redesign dissolves the bonds that hold humans together and runs to the Terror.
Adorno and Horkheimer's count says reason recognizing no authority becomes reason as domination.
The postcolonial count, the heaviest, says the universal yardstick was a ranking device that licensed scientific racism and made Haiti pay an indemnity.
The honest verdict: not achievements or crimes but an unfinished argument with itself the reader is already inside.
